evidence_quote: blue eye + L*>=median skin; confirmed (Scandinavian/E-Asian light skin)
author_explanation_quote: Six missense OCA2 variants were observed...rs74653330:T and rs121918166:T could potentially explain blue eye colour...This is in line with previous findings that this variant is associated with skin pigmentation variation in East Asians and Scandinavians
notes: Missense (coding) variant in OCA2 with functional/phenotypic evidence (eye AND skin colour effect replicated across populations); classified mechanism_proposed on the strength of the coding-variant + cross-population replication account, though authors do not describe the specific protein-level biochemical consequence.

locus_id: OCA2/HERC2 intronic rs72714116 novel blue-eye candidate
classification_notes: Both canonical; phenotypic pattern noted in only 4 individuals, unreplicated -- variant-mechanism gap.
author_explanation_quote: Individuals with rs72714116:T had the same haplotype pair...Our results indicate that the variant rs72714116:T may be an example of a marker for non-blue/non-brown eye colours...However, this was observed in only four individuals, and the findings needs to be replicated...Although the functional role of these variants in eye colour formation is still unknown
notes: Phenotypic pattern (intermediate PIE-score) noted but explicitly no molecular mechanism given; grouped in the closing 'functional role...still unknown' statement.

locus_id: OCA2/HERC2 intronic rs191109490 novel blue-eye candidate
classification_notes: Both canonical; the ONE exception with a SCREEN-predicted cis-regulatory element in HERC2 foreskin melanocytes, regulatory effect on OCA2 expression hypothesized -- still a canonical-gene attribution (both genes on the list).
author_explanation_quote: rs191109490 is contained in a distal enhancer-like signature in HERC2 in foreskin melanocytes, indicating a regulatory effect on OCA2 expression and thereby, pigmentation. However, the specific role of rs191109490 in the cCRE is yet to be identified
notes: Distinct from the other 4 novel candidates: this is the one variant with a SCREEN-predicted candidate cis-regulatory element (cCRE) annotation, giving a concrete (if incompletely specified) regulatory hypothesis -- classified mechanism_proposed while the other four remain stated_unknown/nearest_gene_only.
